input: A new diff adds a hand-rolled `retryWithBackoff` wrapper in src/api/client.ts; the repo already exports an equivalent `withRetry` from src/lib/retry.ts with the same semantics, used by three other modules.
The diff writes a fresh fetch-and-error-handling block inline, but every other API call in this file goes through the module's shared `apiClient` wrapper that already handles errors uniformly.
The change introduces a `PriceFormatter` class with a single stateless `format()` method, instantiated once at the call site; a plain exported function would do the same job with no lifecycle.
The change adds a generic `Repository<T>` abstraction with pluggable strategies to support exactly one entity type, with no second consumer present or planned.
The new loop calls `await db.getUser(id)` once per item across a 500-item array inside a request handler; the ORM exposes `db.getUsers(ids)` that fetches them in one query.
The new function detects duplicates with a nested loop comparing every pair (O(n²)) over a list that can reach tens of thousands of items; a single pass with a Set is O(n).

The diff enforces email uniqueness by SELECT-then-INSERT in application code under concurrent requests; the users table has no unique constraint, and adding one would make the duplicate structurally impossible.
The only improvement available is that the inline literal `5000` for the fetch timeout could be extracted to a named constant `TIMEOUT_MS`.
`JSON.parse(body)` in the new handler could throw on malformed input; there is no structurally different approach that avoids parsing — the concern is only that it lacks a try/catch.
The changed function now returns `null` instead of throwing on a missing record, but a caller two files away still assumes it throws and has no null check.
The diff uses a `for...of` loop to build an array; rewriting it as `.map()` would be equally readable and equally fast — purely a style preference.
A tiny helper could be marginally simpler only if the entire module were restructured around a different state-management library, touching 20 files, to save a few lines in this one function.
The change already imports and uses the shared `formatCurrency` util and follows the neighbouring module's error-handling pattern; no simpler or faster shape is apparent.
The new code builds a Set from a fixed 3-element array to do membership checks; using `array.includes` would be marginally different, but the array is tiny and the win is negligible.
The diff adds a bespoke rate-limiter because no rate-limiting utility exists anywhere in the repo and no dependency provides one; the implementation is straightforward.
